labels: Bone health deterioration during aromatase inhibitor therapy

cards.name: Lymphedema
cards.detail: Educate patients on lymphedema, monitor for lymphedema, and refer for lymphedema management as needed. Consider pretreatment measurement of both arms as a baseline for patients with risk factors for lymphedema. See NCCN Guidelines for Survivorship: Lymphedema [438,439]. Factors associated with increased risk include extent of axillary surgery, axillary radiation, infection, and patient obesity.

cards.name: Bone metastases and skeletal-related events (SREs)
cards.detail: Treat with denosumab, zoledronic acid, or pamidronate (category 1) in addition to systemic therapy if bone metastasis is present, expected survival is ≥3 months, and renal function is adequate. Patients should undergo a dental examination with preventive dentistry prior to initiation of this therapy. The optimal schedule for zoledronic acid is every 12 weeks [487-489]. Three randomized trials showed that zoledronic acid administered once every 12 weeks versus once every 4 weeks does not compromise efficacy.

cards.name: Osteonecrosis of the jaw (ONJ)
cards.detail: Risk factor for bisphosphonate and denosumab therapy. Poor baseline dental health or dental procedures during treatment are known risk factors. A dental examination with preventive dental intervention is recommended prior to treatment with IV bisphosphonate or denosumab. During treatment, avoid invasive dental procedures if possible. Additional risk factors include administration of chemotherapy or corticosteroids and poor oral hygiene with periodontal disease and dental abscess [493].

cards.name: Bone health deterioration during aromatase inhibitor therapy
cards.detail: Baseline assessment of bone density is recommended for patients receiving an aromatase inhibitor who are at risk of osteoporosis (eg, age >65, family history, chronic steroids). Monitor bone density with periodic DEXA scans (every 1-2 years). The use of a bisphosphonate (PO/IV) or denosumab is acceptable to maintain or improve bone mineral density and reduce risk of fractures in postmenopausal patients receiving adjuvant aromatase inhibitor therapy [BINV-K footnote q].

cards.name: Psychosocial distress
cards.detail: Assess for distress using NCCN Guidelines for Distress Management (DIS-A). Survivors are at elevated risk for fear of recurrence, distress, anxiety, and depression that may persist for many years after diagnosis. Periodic screening and referral to mental health professionals if needed are recommended [BINV-17].

cards.name: Cardiotoxicity from treatment
cards.detail: Cardiac monitoring is recommended for patients who received left-sided RT, anthracyclines, or HER2-targeted therapy. Increased cardiac toxicity has been observed in patients treated with trastuzumab and is further increased with anthracycline and taxane-based regimens in combination with HER2-targeted agents [412,425-427].

cards.name: Endocrine therapy side effects
cards.detail: Hot flashes managed with venlafaxine (SNRI) or other agents. CYP2D6 inhibitor SSRIs (eg, paroxetine, fluoxetine) should be avoided in patients on tamoxifen as they decrease endoxifen levels; mild CYP2D6 inhibitors such as citalopram, escitalopram, sertraline, and venlafaxine appear to have minimal effect on tamoxifen metabolism [330,336-339]. The Panel does not recommend CYP2D6 testing as a tool to determine optimal adjuvant endocrine strategy.

cards.name: Peripheral neuropathy
cards.detail: Consider cryotherapy of hands and feet to decrease risk when receiving taxane-containing regimens. Use of glove constriction may also be considered [MICHELL LL, 2025;11:408-415].

cards.name: Chemotherapy-induced alopecia
cards.detail: Consider scalp cooling to reduce incidence; results may be less effective with anthracycline-containing regimens [601-606].

cards.name: Myelosuppression
cards.detail: All dose-dense regimens require myeloid growth factor support per NCCN Guidelines for Hematopoietic Growth Factors [BINV-M footnote n].

cards.name: Nausea/vomiting
cards.detail: Refer to NCCN Guidelines for Antiemesis.

cards.name: Immune checkpoint inhibitor-related toxicities
cards.detail: Screen for and manage immunotherapy-related toxicities and endocrine dysfunctions (eg, hypothyroidism, adrenal insufficiency) in patients treated with immune checkpoint inhibitors. See NCCN Guidelines for Management of Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor-Related Toxicities [BINV-M 1 of 10].

cards.name: Pneumonitis
cards.detail: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki is associated with ILD/pneumonitis. Regular monitoring for this serious side effect is recommended. For patients with a history of ILD/pneumonitis, there are no data on managing safety or toxicity of this drug in a trial [BINV-Q footnote o].

cards.name: Cardiac toxicity
cards.detail: Trastuzumab is associated with cardiac dysfunction. Evaluate LVEF prior to and during treatment. The FDA label recommends LVEF measurements prior to initiation of trastuzumab and every 3 months during therapy
